edges.ev: ra1 expression is reduced in ra2 mutants; ra2 acts upstream of ra1
RA3 functions through the predicted transcriptional regulator RAMOSA1 (RA1)
RA1 imposes determinacy on spikelet pair meristems
REL2 and RA1 physically interact through CTLH domain; RA1 recruits REL2 via EAR motifs
rel2;ra1-RS double mutants had highly branched ears
The ear is usually unbranched, is highly branched in ra1 mutants
rel2;ra2 double mutant shows enhanced ear branching relative to single mutants
RA3 encodes a trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase expressed in discrete domains subtending axillary inflorescence meristems
RA3 regulates inflorescence branching by modification of a sugar signal that moves into axillary meristems
RA1 binds KN1's regulatory intron; RA1 and KN1 share 189 overlapping target peaks
FEA4 binds and activates RA1 which imposes determinacy on axillary branches
rel2 ears showed increased kernel rows, with IM size ~16% enlarged relative to wild-type
rel2 ears showed increased kernel rows
spikelet-pair meristem determinacy sets the ordered rows; loss leads to branching, reduced rows

perturbations.ev: The ear, which is usually unbranched, is highly branched in ra1 mutants, resulting in reduced fertility
ra2 loss-of-function gives abnormal branching in ears (reduced/disordered rows)
in ra3 plants the inflorescence branching pattern is disturbed, with ears producing abnormally shaped long branches at their bases
rel2 ears showed increased kernel rows with ~16% enlarged IM
rel2;ra1-RS double mutants had highly branched ears; spikelet-pair meristems convert to branch meristems
rel2;ra2 double mutant shows enhanced ear branching relative to single mutants
massive proliferation of branches with primary, secondary, and tertiary indeterminate structures
